title: Is there an Evangelion Backlash in fandom
display_title: Is there an Evangelion Backlash in fandom
wikitext:
[]: Is there an "Evangelion Backlash" in fandom?             Quote Post by Incisivis » Thu Jul 03, 2008 6:44 pm I start new topics very rarely. Let's see if I can *not* botch it. I'm still a newbie when it comes to anime, being part of the Pokemon generation and all, plus slowing my growth by keeping interest in Western animation and other things. But expressions of outright loathing of Evangelion, and stereotyping NGE fans as those who've been fooled by calculated obfuscation into believing in depth...I've often felt that these things have been on the rise since I became interested in the series in 2002. The aforementioned attacks on the fans themselves are what disturbs me just a little, because I don't see that sort of thing too often in fandom (I travel in pretty subdued circles), while attacks on a series are just as normal as anything. So...am I just stupidly hypersensitive, or have the more experienced noticed this, too? Feel free to discuss the reasons, if it turns out that I'm not crazy.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Jul 03, 2008 7:02 pm Being a newer Eva man myself (I appeared on the scene in 2006), I noticed this sort of problem too. Whenever I ask other anime fans if they like/have seen Eva, most say they have and or say it's an excellent series.... But their have been a few (espeically online) who tend to say it's "pretentious" or "crappy for not explaining wtf was going on in the ending(s)", things like that. I haven't really noticed fans thinking Eva fans are superior or bullshit like that, except I few places online and I can't even remember where right now. All I know is, I find it odd myself considering other anime series don't seem to be as criticized as harshly as Eva is. I get the feeling Eva's treated in a polarizing way that's similar to how fans view Shinji: You either love it or you don't.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Thu Jul 03, 2008 7:26 pm I'm also a newer fan. Started watching the show in 2005. And I'm just a fan. If people like it, hey that's cool. If they don't like it, that's cool too. People have their own opinions. There's always gonna be somebody that doesn't like something. But this might be a bit too much. Brik is awesome for being part of it, though.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Thu Jul 03, 2008 7:35 pm People are probably fed up with it being so popular. After repeating, "Evangelion is Great!" like parrots for a while, they think about the series themselves and then say "Nah, I don't like it...actually, I'm sick of it!" I only suppose it's natural. People might feel the same way about Haruhi or Code Geass another five or ten years down the road.

[]:             Quote Post by TheAyanamiOtaku » Thu Jul 03, 2008 8:55 pm Im a newer fan than the people who claimed to be new fans/stroking_ego Finished the series and movies a month before I joined this forum. - When I told my other friends that I watched Evangelion AND I love it, I get a generous helping of weird stares and outrage.Here is a montage of all of my classmates' responses. Or rather, all of them I can remember. "Evangelion is so old and uncool and doesn't make any freakin' sense at all." "Dude, the graphics suck, and the girls are ugly and weird!" "That shit was confusing." "You're a fan of Evangerie-on? What the hell, dude get away from me." "I don't want to see you ever again." "Yeah I don't like it I think it's a pathetic copy of Gundam." "Eww, that anime is so weird. It's like the director is trying to give you a stroke, ha ha ha...why haven't you watched Bleach yet?" "It's old? So that means it sucks right? Bad graphics and cheesy storylines? Yeah?" "What the hell?!" "Evangelion is totally like fifty years ago, Johnathan, get with the fucking program...It's ALL about Bleach, Naruto, Avatar, and Death Note! You know what you're hopeless I'm going to walk away from you get away from me." "Wow. Are you gay? I remember there was a gay scene somewhere." "Johnathan maybe you should watch an anime that makes SENSE! And doesn't change the storyline every fucking episode." "Yeah I watched it too. I loved the part where the camera focused on the boobs. Haha. But there weren't enough sex scenes." "No, it's so violent!"

[]:             Quote Post by Gamer_2k4 » Thu Jul 03, 2008 10:03 pm TheAyanamiOtaku wrote: Im a newer fan than the people who claimed to be new fans/stroking_ego Finished the series and movies a month before I joined this forum.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Thu Jul 03, 2008 10:27 pm Explanation 1: Noobs ruin everything. (Irony? no, I've been an EVA fan since 2000... rebuild brought me back) Anything that was made before they became fans is assumed by the swelling tide of late-comers to not be very good, because if it was then obviously they would have become fans earlier. Explanation 2: Idiocracy is correct... people really are getting dumber and lazier. Soon all shows will spend the first half hour recapping the last episode so the audience can keep up, and anything more than an episode old will not be considered important for continuity. Plotlines with any degree of subtlety will feature a scrolling bar of explanatory text at the bottom of the screen to keep the dense from feeling left out. Explanation 3: Rampant graphics-whoreism. Rebuild will assuage this issue for approximately seventeen minutes before it is declared to be out of touch with current styles and discussed only as a passing influence upon the next wave of shows to be featured on Adult Swim.

[]:             Quote Post by Iron Rooster » Thu Jul 03, 2008 10:42 pm You know, it just might have something to do with the fact that a lot of people don't see anime fans in a good light. Add to that that Eva fans have a tendency to be 'different' from the rest of the pack and you get some interesting reactions. Yeah, and I saw Eva a couple of years ago my junior year in high school when the dubs were on Adult Swim ( I don't give a shit about the dub-sub war, I'll take either ). Ha, it's kind of funny that the first episode I saw was number 19.

[]:             Quote Post by ZapX » Thu Jul 03, 2008 10:54 pm It's not really that uncommon. People take their in-group/out-group thinking a little far pretty regularly. See also: Race, Religion, Operating Systems, EGF, &c.

[]:             Quote Post by Defectron » Thu Jul 03, 2008 11:21 pm "Evangelion is so old and uncool and doesn't make any freakin' sense at all." "Dude, the graphics suck, and the girls are ugly and weird!" "That shit was confusing." "You're a fan of Evangerie-on? What the hell, dude get away from me." "I don't want to see you ever again." "Yeah I don't like it I think it's a pathetic copy of Gundam." "Eww, that anime is so weird. It's like the director is trying to give you a stroke, ha ha ha...why haven't you watched Bleach yet?" "It's old? So that means it sucks right? Bad graphics and cheesy storylines? Yeah?" "What the hell?!" "Evangelion is totally like fifty years ago, Johnathan, get with the fucking program...It's ALL about Bleach, Naruto, Avatar, and Death Note! You know what you're hopeless I'm going to walk away from you get away from me." "Wow. Are you gay? I remember there was a gay scene somewhere." "Johnathan maybe you should watch an anime that makes SENSE! And doesn't change the storyline every fucking episode." "Yeah I watched it too. I loved the part where the camera focused on the boobs. Haha. But there weren't enough sex scenes." "No, it's so violent!"

[]:             Quote Post by Iron Rooster » Thu Jul 03, 2008 11:52 pm ^ I'm in the group that hates most.

[]:             Quote Post by iamsynch » Fri Jul 04, 2008 12:15 am UrsusArctos wrote: People are probably fed up with it being so popular. After repeating, "Evangelion is Great!" like parrots for a while, they think about the series themselves and then say "Nah, I don't like it...actually, I'm sick of it!" I only suppose it's natural. People might feel the same way about Haruhi or Code Geass another five or ten years down the road.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Fri Jul 04, 2008 12:45 am Iron Rooster wrote: ^I'm in the group that hates most.

[]:             Quote Post by TheAyanamiOtaku » Fri Jul 04, 2008 12:52 am Gamer_2k4 wrote: Pft, step off. I started watching in late April of this year.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Fri Jul 04, 2008 1:06 am The attitude on /a/ had tended to be "Lol, deep" -- but since the Rebuild 1.0 rips (DVD especially) have been out, there've been a bunch of threads that could well have appeared here. Of course there's always been a background "ZOMG, TEH Rei" and"She is so cute, that is why she is called 'Donate to 4chan-chan'."

[]:             Quote Post by p06 » Fri Jul 04, 2008 8:32 am A lot of my friends cannot identify with the emotional and psychological problems of the characters. They then assume that anyone who likes the series must be able to identify with these problems, and therefore must be weird/emo. Which i admit is true to a certain extent.

[]:             Quote Post by V » Fri Jul 04, 2008 11:03 am well Incisivis, ANY popular series has some backlash, then gets "rediscovered" again after it is no longer in vogue to hate on it That said......Evangelion is a pretty deep series and its "art" but it was released as a popular TV series. LOTR was a book first, but Eva really didn't get enough "professional" attention; I mean the result was a disproportionately large number of "Rei fanboys" make up "Eva fandom" and didn't even bother to watch every episode

[]:             Quote Post by TheAyanamiOtaku » Fri Jul 04, 2008 1:37 pm but I evolved. and I found my way here...(lol)

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Fri Jul 04, 2008 3:36 pm iamsynch wrote: UrsusArctos wrote: People are probably fed up with it being so popular. After repeating, "Evangelion is Great!" like parrots for a while, they think about the series themselves and then say "Nah, I don't like it...actually, I'm sick of it!" I only suppose it's natural. People might feel the same way about Haruhi or Code Geass another five or ten years down the road.

[]:             Quote Post by Incisivis » Fri Jul 04, 2008 4:44 pm All right, let me elaborate. Do you feel there is an Evangelion backlash besides the fact that a series like it inevitably creates polarization? That is, and I know I should have written it this way to begin with, has there been an overall hardening of reaction to NGE within anime fandom in the last few years? That these days it's pretty much all cynicism whenever NGE is brought up, after years of it being adored? I don't want it to sound like I think no one should crticize NGE, but it seemed that when I was first getting into anime, the overall fandom attitude/approach to Evangelion was a lot more balanced. Furthermore, I like quite a few polarizing things, and I've never seen this sort of blatant anger that's sometimes directed at NGE, i.e. "Anyone who likes this is an emo teen!" sort of thing, who seem to find it impossible that NGE fans could have a sense of humour or self-awareness. ...not that I'd be the best counter-example, but am I at least a few inches closer to clarifying?
